message:
fix(decomposing-matrix): transpose nalgebra eigenvectors into row-major layout

DecomposingPositiveMatrix::decompose() stores the eigenvector
matrix from nalgebra's SymmetricEigen into `self.eigenbasis` for
later consumption via the crate-local `idx(n, row, col)`
row-major indexer. The previous one-liner —

    self.eigenbasis = eig.eigenvectors.as_slice().to_vec();

copied nalgebra's COLUMN-major buffer verbatim into a buffer
that every subsequent read interprets as ROW-major. The matrix
landed transposed, so every later call through the crate-local
`idx` — most importantly the inverse-square-root computation
`s = sum_k U[k,i] * U[k,j] / sqrt(lambda_k)` — ended up summing
over the wrong axis and effectively returning U^T D U instead of
U D U^T. That's *not* a sign of C^{-1/2}, and the product
`invsqrt^T invsqrt` no longer recovered C^{-1} for any non-
identity covariance.

Concrete demonstration: for C = [[2, 1], [1, 3]], the analytic
inverse is (1/5) * [[3, -1], [-1, 2]] = [[0.6, -0.2], [-0.2, 0.4]].
Running invsqrt * invsqrt through the un-transposed code produced
the same diagonal entries but sign-flipped off-diagonals
([[0.6, +0.2], [+0.2, 0.4]]), because U D U^T and U^T D U differ
only on the off-diagonal block for a non-diagonal C. Downstream
CMA-ES steps that depend on this (Mahalanobis distance under the
current covariance, sampling from C) were quietly biased.

Fix: after computing `eig`, resize self.eigenbasis to n*n and
write each eigenvector entry into its row-major slot explicitly:

    for i in 0..n { for k in 0..n {
        self.eigenbasis[idx(n, i, k)] = eig.eigenvectors[(i, k)];
    }}

The (i, k) access goes through nalgebra's own 2D indexer, which
handles the column-major translation internally, so the right-
hand side sees element U[i, k] regardless of storage order. Then
we write it into the row-major `idx(n, i, k)` slot the rest of
the crate reads.

Regression test `invsqrt_squared_equals_c_inverse` constructs
C = [[2, 1], [1, 3]], decomposes it, materializes invsqrt,
computes invsqrt^T * invsqrt, and asserts entrywise closeness
to the analytic C^{-1}. Before this fix, the off-diagonal signs
flipped and the test failed; after, it passes with |delta| <
1e-12. A long explanatory doc comment above the transposing loop
in decompose() and on the test itself pins the invariant so a
future refactor that walks the one-liner back hits both a
compile-time reviewer hint AND a failing test.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
